document_identity.parent: McDonald's Corporation (Delaware corporation; also the predecessor)

item1_structural.entity_structure: Wholly-owned subsidiary of McDonald's Corporation; formed 2004, assets transferred 2005
item1_structural.franchise_mix_description: ~95% of U.S. restaurants franchised to independent operators; ~5% operated by McOpCo subsidiaries

pending_cases_noted: Deslandes v. McDonald's (no-poach antitrust; remanded to district court after SCOTUS cert denied)
Crawford et al. v. McDonald's (77 former Black franchisees, discrimination) + related McPherson complaint
Kytch, Inc. v. McDonald's (trade libel, tortious interference)
Michell et al. v. McDonald's (CT franchisee discrimination; partial dismissal Sep 2025)
Williams v. McDonald's (E. coli Quarter Pounder class action, filed Oct 31, 2024)
Le et al. v. McDonald's (McRib deceptive marketing class action, filed Dec 23, 2025)
McDonald's Corp v. Rusi Pestonjee (collection action, Sri Lanka, 2024)
item3_litigation.concluded_cases_summary: Extensive list including multiple franchisee buyouts: Lentini $22M, Byrd Bros. $6.5M, Washington $33.5M, Ochoa CA joint-employer $3.75M, Puerto Rico law-75 settlements $15.78M and $6.32M, Tavarua $15.6M. Also SEC Easterbrook administrative settlement (no monetary penalty, Jan 2023) and PFAS settlement $145K.

item5_initial_fees.refund_policy: Entire initial fee refundable only if restaurant construction is not completed within 1 year of signing. No refunds otherwise.
item5_initial_fees.rebuild_relocation_credit: Prorated based on prior fee; credit applied on the earlier of (a) first of month after 7th year post-reopening, or (b) end of previous franchise term

item6_ongoing_fees.royalty_rate_trigger: 5% applies on/after Jan 1, 2024 for (i) new restaurants, (ii) McOpCo sales, (iii) franchisor ROFR resales. 4% persists for family transfers, new-term agreements on existing restaurants, inter-franchisee resales, and rebuilds of existing locations.
item6_ongoing_fees.advertising_recipient: Not paid to franchisor (local co-ops + OPNAD)
item6_ongoing_fees.ad_fund_audit_note: OPNAD fund independently audited annually; local co-op audit practices vary
item6_ongoing_fees.rent_structure: Monthly Base Rent (based on franchisor's investment in land + building × finance factor, + 3rd-party landlord pass-thru) + Pass Thru Rent (lease escalations from head-landlord) + Percentage Rent (generally 6%–23% of Gross Sales; minimum 11.50% of Gross Sales from year 8)
item6_ongoing_fees.rent_term_cap: Total change in Percentage Rent over term generally capped at 3.50 percentage points absolute, unless needed to hit 11.50% floor
item6_ongoing_fees.co_investment_terms: Optional rent reduction in 0.25% 'Quarters'; cost per Quarter ≥ $30,000; detailed criteria in Item 6 Note 4
item6_ongoing_fees.technology_fee_summary: Annual tech/SaaS stack includes Sesame $1,133 + $2,600 one-time, Global Mobile App $664, McDelivery POS $620, Self-Ordering Kiosk $1,500 one-time + $558 annual, eProduction $47, Back Office Integration $545, Payments/Fraud Mgmt $740, Employee Engagement $385, Deployment/OTP/Support $2,529, Restaurant Network Mgmt $1,134, Restaurant Hardware Mgmt $951, RFM $690, Microsoft License $707, Edge $435 + $120 one-time, GRNT $185 + $491 one-time, DMB 2.0 $264 one-time, Store Mail $56.40. Optional: HHOT $50 + $500 one-time, PDW $167, McD Connect $250, Experience Survey $62, Pricing Engine $264.

item8_supply_chain.gift_card_system_provider: P2W, Inc. NFP (independent non-profit; board includes McDonald's employees and franchisees)

principal_marks.mark: McDONALD'S and Golden Arches Logo (sign design)

item13_trademarks.owner: Restaurant Brands, LLC (affiliate of McDonald's Corporation)

termination_by_franchisor_with_cause_tri: failure to maintain restaurant to standards
any amount owing to franchisor unpaid >30 days
item17_legal_mechanics.death_disability_franchisor_option_to_pu: Yes, after 1 year of franchisor management

item19_fpr.fpr_type: Pro forma OIBO at three sales bands (single construct)
item19_fpr.population_pro_forma_scope: Independent franchisee traditional restaurants open ≥1 year, excluding McOpCo, Satellites, and 2024 change-of-owner restaurants

item20_outlets.confidentiality_clause_note: 'In some instances, current and former franchisees sign provisions restricting their ability to speak openly about their experience with McDonald's.' — explicit gag-clause flag in Item 20 narrative.

statements_provided: Consolidated Statement of Income (FY2024, FY2023, FY2022)
Consolidated Statement of Members' Equity (3 years)
Consolidated Statement of Cash Flows (3 years)
key_financial_observations: Material related-party exposure: Due to parent (current) $3,310.6M; royalty-to-parent expense $1,069.4M in FY2024.
Retained earnings unchanged YoY ($4,633.9M) — effectively full upstream of net income to parent via distributions/inter-company.
Lease obligations dominate the liability side: current + long-term lease liability $6,044.9M (~27% of total assets).
Goodwill $1,498.7M is modest relative to $22.2B asset base.
Asset composition heavily weighted to net P&E ($13.08B) + ROU assets ($5.84B) — real estate is the economic engine, not brand.

special_risks.detail: Franchise agreement requires mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation only in Illinois. May force franchisee to accept less favorable settlements and/or incur higher dispute costs.

risk_flags_early: NO right to renew or extend — 'New Term' is discretionary franchisor policy, not a contract right
NO exclusive or protected territory; encroachment contractually permitted
Franchisor owns/controls real estate; rent is the dominant economic lever, layered on top of royalty
Percentage rent up to 23% of Gross Sales + escalation floor of 11.50% from year 8
Required purchases ~55–65% of ongoing and ~90–95% of initial purchases
Sesame POS, Cashless 3.0, and Gift Card System are effectively mandatory; franchisor holds uncapped independent access to transaction-level data
Post-term non-compete: 18 months, 10 miles (plus during term blanket prohibition)
Illinois venue + Illinois governing law (out-of-state risk flag)
Multiple pending discrimination lawsuits by current/former franchisees
Pending E. coli class action (Quarter Pounder, Oct 2024)
Material related-party exposure (Due to parent $3.31B); retained earnings unchanged YoY
113 franchisees departed in most recent fiscal year (Exhibit S)
Confidentiality/gag clauses flagged — some franchisees contractually restricted from speaking openly about their experience

extraction_meta.extraction_method: Claude-first text-layer reading via pagetext dump
extraction_meta.coverage_qualifier: Items 1-23 narrative and all core tables fully extracted from pages 1-60; exhibit decoding limited to Exhibit A (Item 21 financials). Exhibits B–T cataloged only.
extraction_meta.prior_benchmarks_excluded: This canonical was built WITHOUT reference to prior McDonald's runs. B1 regression check will be the first cross-reference.
